user_message: How should I coach Tom Rodriguez on Lucerne Publishing? We had a deep-dive last month -- need the follow-up.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Laura Anderson <laura.anderson@lucernepubli.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-05 09:35 EST
Subject: Re: Re: deployment blockers Timeline

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with deployment blockers. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

---
From: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
To: Laura Anderson <laura.anderson@lucernepubli.com>
Date: 2024-02-20 14:10 EST
Subject: URGENT: deployment blockers Issue at Lucerne Publishing

Just checking in on the deployment blockers proposal. Our Head of Digital is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Laura Anderson <laura.anderson@lucernepubli.com>
To: Tom Rodriguez <tom.rodriguez@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-02 15:58 EST
Subject: Re: Re: deployment blockers Timeline

Our team encountered some challenges with deployment blockers. Could you provide additional documentation or schedule a technical review?

Meeting: deployment blockers Discussion - Lucerne Publishing
Date: 2024-01-15
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing), Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft), Priya (Lucerne Publishing)

[00:02:00] Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on deployment blockers from our last session.

[00:08:00] Priya (Lucerne Publishing): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about data residency. Can we address those?

[00:10:00] Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing): On the deployment blockers front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around data residency.

[00:14:00] Laura Anderson (Lucerne Publishing): Let me add some context -- we've been working on stakeholder alignment and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:16:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Our CTO reviewed this and has concerns about budget approval. Can we address those?

[00:21:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): The timeline for stakeholder alignment is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:25:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): Let me add some context -- we've been working on integration complexity and found that the existing infrastructure won't support the target workload without upgrades.

[00:27:00] Tom Rodriguez (Microsoft): On the deployment blockers front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around timeline constraints.

[00:31:00] Priya (Lucerne Publishing): Our VP reviewed this and has concerns about budget approval. Can we address those?

usability_anchors: Score 5: Complete, well-structured output matching the Pre-Meeting Brief template with all required artifacts present and actionable
Score 3: Output covers main requirements but missing one or two secondary artifacts or lacking specificity
Score 1: Output is generic, missing key artifacts, or fails to address the specific request
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct deployment blockers decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
